heroHeadline: Trump-Xi May Summit Confirmed Amid Tariff War; US Strikes Kharg Island — 90% Iran Oil; Trump 'Civilization Will Die' Ultimatum; $6.6T Market Crash; China 34% Tariff; DOGE Crashes SSA Servers
heroSubtitle: US forces struck Kharg Island on April 7 — handling ~90% of Iran's oil exports — as Trump issued an apocalyptic 8pm ET deadline: 'A whole civilization will die tonight.' Iran threatened to target Gulf Arab oil infrastructure in retaliation. Separately, the April 3–4 tariff market crash — the largest two-day market cap loss in US history at $6.6 trillion — was triggered by China's 34% retaliatory tariff on all US goods, with the Dow losing ~9.5% over two days and the Nasdaq entering bear market territory. DOGE caused a Social Security Administration server crash on April 7 after deploying untested software. New 50% metal tariff duties took effect April 6–7 under Section 232 authority. Trump's approval remains below 40%.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, SCOTUSblog, NPR, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Bloomberg, CNBC.
